page: Amazon Web Services t3.medium is a burstable general-purpose instance type built on the AWS Nitro System. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks place the t3.medium in the lower tier of cloud compute resources, with CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently scoring in the bottom 10% of tested servers. The instance utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, meaning it is designed to handle temporary spikes in workload but will experience throttled performance once its CPU credit balance is depleted. This makes it unsuitable for sustained high-compute tasks, heavy database operations, or large-scale LLM inference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3.medium serves as a low-cost entry point for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization. It is structurally optimized for resource density and budget-conscious deployments rather than raw performance. Appropriate workloads include microservices, low-traffic web servers, development and testing environments, and small code repositories where CPU utilization remains low or intermittent.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.medium is a burstable general-purpose instance utilizing the AWS Nitro System. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results for this instance, including CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations, fall within the bottom 10% of performance tiers. This reflects its design as a budget-friendly, burstable resource rather than a high-performance compute engine. The t3.medium is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that experience occasional spikes. It is best suited for development environments, low-traffic web serving, and lightweight microservices.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.medium is a burstable general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, designed for low-cost, intermittent workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3.medium is a burstable instance with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM, ideal for low-cost development and low-traffic web serving.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable instance offering dual virtual cores and DDR4 memory for lightweight, intermittent workloads and development environments.
